relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Grace is not an excuse to remain in misalignment.
Those who died to misalignment cannot keep living inside its rule.
Baptism into Messiah is baptism into his death.
Buried with him, we walk in newness of life.
Union with his death opens union with resurrection.
The old human is crucified so misalignment’s body loses rule.
Death breaks the claim of sin’s regime.
Dying with Messiah opens living with him.
Death no longer rules the risen Messiah.
His death is once for all; his life is toward the Divine.
Count yourself dead to misalignment and alive to the Divine.
Do not let misalignment reign through mortal desire.
Offer the body as instrument of justice, alive from the dead.
Misalignment will not rule you because grace is your field.
Grace does not authorize returning to misalignment.
What you obey becomes the power you serve.
The heart obeys a new pattern of teaching.
Released from sin’s rule, you belong to justice.
The same embodied members once given to lawlessness are now given to sanctification.
Sin’s freedom is freedom from justice, not true freedom.
The fruit of the old way ends in death.
Released into the Divine, fruit ripens into sanctification and age-long life.
Misalignment pays death; the Divine gives life as gift in Messiah.

contemplative_note: Romans 6 is embodied liberation. The body is not hated; the body becomes an instrument of justice instead of a weapon of misalignment.
